text:
There was an old oak tree who knew everything. One autumn afternoon, an old lady passed by. She stopped and stared at the tree. It was old. Very old. Old oak trees shouldn trees didn't grow old. Maybe the old lady thought it didn't.

The old oak tree spoke. "You think I'm old? You think I don't know anything? You think I don't know anything?

That's hardly fair!

You think I don't know anything? Really?

It all began when the oak tree was just a sapling. It knew everything that happened. It was informed about every firefly's dream. It knew everything that happened at every wedding.

But one thing it didn't know?

It didn't know anything.

That's right. When mankind came... well, there wasn't really anything they could do to save it. So they chopped down the old oak tree.

What?

That old oak tree?

That old oak tree.

It knew everything.

It knew everything
